extraction.one_line_summary: THOMAS Geraint attacked final corner at Alpe d'Huez in yellow jersey, dropped DUMOULIN Tom with explosive acceleration on 6-7% grade to win.
tactical_patterns: THOMAS Geraint carried absurd cornering speed into final bend then accelerated 25-30mph on 6-7% grade, sprinter power trumping climbers.
KRUIJSWIJK Steven's 6+ minute solo breakaway nearly succeeded; Sky pacing miscalculated time gaps badly due to switchback illusions.
BERNAL Egan at 21 singlehandedly controlled Sky train, chasing back NIBALI Vincenzo and QUINTANA Nairo attacks before veteran teammates needed to respond.
LATOUR Pierre survived QUADFAIRE attack to retain white jersey despite looking cooked; GC stamina depth deceived immediate visual assessment.

rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: second consecutive Alpe stage win reveals peak explosive power while in yellow, not riding domestique for FROOME Chris despite claims.
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: positive: controlled Sky tempo solo at 21 years old, 4:41 behind white jersey suggests massive upside for Pyrenees targeting.
rider_signals.KRUIJSWIJK Steven: positive: 6-minute solo advantage deep into Alpe d'Huez shows exceptional diesel engine when given free rein from distance.
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: negative: crash before finale destroyed certain stage win opportunity, timing shows vulnerability when isolated without teammates.
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: faded badly to 9th after BERNAL Egan singlehandedly neutralized his attacks, troubling sign for Colombian's form trajectory.
rider_signals.DUMOULIN Tom: neutral: chased THOMAS Geraint to line for 2nd but lacked final-corner acceleration despite climbing 15,000 feet strongly.
